hypothesis.original_question: On silicon heterojunction (SHJ) solar cells, a sub-100 nm electroplated nickel barrier followed by electroplated copper produces a front-grid metallization with contact resistivity ≤ 2 mΩ·cm² and no measurable efficiency loss after 1000 h damp-heat aging (85°C / 85% RH) — matching screen-printed silver at <10% of the metallization cost.

refined.intervention: Replace the screen-printed silver front-grid on a commercial SHJ M6 cell with a NOBLE-style stack: PVD-Cu(50 nm)/Al(100 nm) seed → inkjet-printed 1 wt% NaOH(aq) selective Al etch → electroplated Ni barrier (80 nm, sub-100 nm hypothesis target) from a sulfamate bath → electroplated Cu (5 µm) from an acid Cu sulfate bath (Atotech Cupracid Ultra-class) → immersion Ag cap (~200 nm). Anneal 200 °C / 15 min for a-Si:H recovery.
refined.outcome: Front-grid metallization that simultaneously satisfies (a) line contact resistivity ρc ≤ 2 mΩ·cm² by TLM at room temperature, (b) cell efficiency degradation Δη ≤ 0.5 %abs after 1000 h damp-heat (IEC 61215, 85°C / 85% RH) on a glass-glass POE-encapsulated mini-module, and (c) ≥1 N/mm 90° peel adhesion at 0 and 1000 h.
refined.threshold: All three metrics must hold simultaneously on Arm A. Failure of any one rejects the hypothesis. Cost target (front-side metallization material + process): ≤ 10 % of the screen-printed Ag baseline cost on a $/cell basis at 5 GW production scale, sensitivity-tested at $25 / oz Ag baseline.
refined.mechanism: Electroplated Cu has bulk resistivity 1.7 µΩ·cm vs Ag 1.6 µΩ·cm — comparable line conductance at equal cross-section. Cu has the highest interstitial diffusivity of any 3d transition metal in Si (D ≈ 3×10⁻⁴ exp(−0.18 eV/kT) cm²/s; traverses 400 µm Si in <11 h at room temperature per Lindroos 2015), creating a deep recombination band at EC−0.15 to EC−0.58 eV that collapses minority-carrier lifetime catastrophically. A continuous Ni layer ≥60 nm forms a NiSi/Ni₃Si silicide barrier that stops Cu in-diffusion at the SHJ-compatible <200°C process temperature; the SHJ TCO (ITO ≥75 nm) is itself a co-barrier. Cheng 2018 STEM-EDS confirmed 60 nm electroplated Ni fails as a Cu barrier at 300°C anneal but holds at 200°C; 120 nm Ni holds to 300°C. The hypothesis's sub-100 nm target sits at the edge of Cheng's window — empirical verification by SIMS at the c-Si interface is required.
refined.control: Arm D: 5–10 cells from the same wafer batch metallized with low-temperature SHJ-grade screen-printed Ag paste (generic class — Heraeus SOL954 / Solamet PV42B / Namics LCT-class — NOT the legacy fire-through PV21A / PV56S which are PERC-only), aged in the same DH chamber simultaneously. Secondary controls: Arm B (Cu-only, no Ni barrier — SunDrive-style, validates TCO-as-barrier alternative); Arm C (Ni 120 nm — maps Cheng threshold under SHJ thermal budget).

novelty.verdict_text: The exact architecture (sub-100 nm electroplated Ni + electroplated Cu on SHJ) overlaps with multiple published demonstrations within ±20 nm Ni and ±2× damp-heat duration. SunDrive Solar achieved 26.41 % certified Cu-plated SHJ at ISFH (Aug 2022) with no Ni barrier; Fraunhofer ISE's NOBLE process reaches 22.6 % on M2 cells with PVD-seed + plated Cu; CSEM mini-modules survive 2000–2700 h damp heat. Where this experiment adds value beyond replication: (a) explicit 4-arm comparison sweeping Ni thickness 0/80/120 nm on the *same* wafer batch maps the Cheng 2018 threshold under SHJ's ≤200°C process budget — a measurement no published study has done; (b) extended damp-heat to 2000 h on EVA vs POE matched laminates per Karas 2022's protocol gives an architecture-agnostic encapsulant decision rule; (c) GridMaster-class cost model anchored to the ITRPV 2024 Ag laydown trajectory and the SunDrive ARENA process step list yields a defensible $/cell number missing from Hatt and Lachowicz.

references.title: SunDrive Copper Metallisation Demonstration Project — ARENA Knowledge Sharing Report
Native Oxide Barrier Layer for Selective Electroplated Metallization of Silicon Heterojunction Solar Cells
references.authors: Hatt, T.; Kluska, S.; Yamin, M.; Bartsch, J.; Glatthaar, M.

references.title: Aging tests of mini-modules with copper-plated heterojunction solar cells
references.authors: Lachowicz, A.; Andreatta, G.; Faes, A.; et al. (CSEM)

summary.pull_quote: The hypothesis's pass/fail line is set not by Cu's bulk conductivity (Cu wins) but by whether a sub-100 nm Ni barrier can suppress sub-ppb Cu in-diffusion under 1000 h of EVA-derived acetic-acid attack — the same failure mode that has kept the silicon photovoltaic industry on silver for forty years.

protocol.title: Wafer sourcing & baseline characterization
protocol.rationale: Procure 60 commercial SHJ M6 precursors (n-type Cz-Si, ~155 µm, with bilateral a-Si:H(i/n) and a-Si:H(i/p) passivation and 80 nm ITO TCO front + rear) from a pilot-line partner (CSEM, Meyer Burger, or LONGi pilot). Baseline pFF, Suns-Voc, and ITO sheet resistance must be uniform across the batch (σ < 1 % on all three) to permit cross-arm comparison. SunDrive ARENA reports the precursor batch is the dominant variance source.

failure_warnings.label: Heterogeneous batch passivation
failure_warnings.mitigation: Reject any wafer with QSSPC iVoc < 740 mV at injection 10¹⁵ cm⁻³ before metallization.

protocol.title: PVD seed deposition (Cu 50 nm / Al 100 nm)
protocol.rationale: Sputter a Cu(50 nm) seed layer onto the front ITO at room temperature, followed by Al(100 nm) cap on top. The Al will be the NOBLE plating mask after selective patterning; its self-passivating native AlOx (formed by 1–5 d ambient hold or 1 d at 50°C / 6 % RH) gives the contact angle 100–120° required for fine-line inkjet etch (Hatt 2019).

failure_warnings.label: Sputter damage to a-Si:H
failure_warnings.mitigation: Soft-sputter mode, low DC bias, sample T < 80°C, 200°C / 15 min anneal post-plate to recover passivation per Hatt 2019.

failure_warnings.label: Inhomogeneous PVD-Al
failure_warnings.mitigation: Verify Al uniformity by reflectometry; reject any wafer with > 5 % thickness variation.

protocol.title: Inkjet-NaOH selective Al etch (NOBLE patterning)
protocol.rationale: Print 1 wt % NaOH(aq) ink (2–5 pL droplets, Notion Systems / SUSS PiXDRO industrial inkjet) along the desired grid pattern (finger pitch 1.4 mm, finger width target 25 µm). NaOH etches Al at 2.4–11 nm/s (1–4 wt % range, Hatt 2019 Fig. 3) but does not attack Cu, Ag, or ITO. Hold 5 min then DI-rinse. The grid lines are now Cu-on-ITO openings; the rest of the cell stays masked by the residual Al + native AlOx.

failure_warnings.label: Ink spreading / lateral etch
failure_warnings.mitigation: Verify AlOx contact angle 100–120° before printing; pre-condition wafers ≥5 d ambient or 1 d 50°C / 6 % RH (Hatt 2019).

failure_warnings.label: ITO attack at over-etch
failure_warnings.mitigation: End-point monitor by reflectometry; over-etch into ITO < 5 nm.

protocol.title: Electroplated Ni barrier (Arm A: 80 nm; Arm C: 120 nm; Arm B: skip)
protocol.rationale: Electroplate Ni from a sulfamate bath onto the exposed Cu seed in the grid openings. Sulfamate Ni is the canonical low-stress, fine-grain electrolyte: Ni(SO₃NH₂)₂ 80 g/L, NiCl₂ 5 g/L, H₃BO₃ 35 g/L, pH 3.8–4.2, T 50°C, J 4 A/dm² cathodic. Plate to 80 nm (Arm A, sub-100 nm hypothesis target) or 120 nm (Arm C, Cheng 2018 safe-threshold). Arm B is skipped — Cu plates directly onto Cu seed. Verify thickness by surface XRF or ellipsometry on a coupon. The Ni must be continuous (no pinholes >1 µm); pinhole-decoration test by 5-min Cu displacement bath before next step is REQUIRED — pinhole density >10 cm⁻² rejects the wafer.

failure_warnings.label: Sub-100 nm Ni below Cheng 60 nm-fails-at-300°C threshold
failure_warnings.mitigation: Limit post-plating thermal budget to ≤200°C / 15 min (Hatt 2019); run pinhole-decoration test (Cu displacement, 5 min) before Cu plate; reject if pinhole density >10 cm⁻².

failure_warnings.label: Cu through pinhole into c-Si bulk
failure_warnings.mitigation: TCO ≥75 nm acts as co-barrier (Yacouba 2025); SIMS at 1000 h DH for verification.

protocol.title: Electroplated Cu thickening (5 µm)
protocol.rationale: Plate Cu from an Atotech Cupracid Ultra-class acid Cu electrolyte (CuSO₄·5H₂O 220 g/L, H₂SO₄ 60 g/L, Cl⁻ 100 ppm, Cupracid brightener/leveler additives 5–10 mL/L) using pulsed forward/reverse current — 6 A/dm² cathodic, 15 ms forward / 1 ms reverse, anodic:cathodic 4.5 (Hatt 2019). Plate to 5 µm finger height (target aspect ratio ≈ 0.6 at 25 µm finger width). Bath temperature 25°C with mild paddle agitation. P-Cu bagged anodes. Aim for line resistance ≤2 µΩ·cm bulk Cu and ≤1 Ω/cm finger.

reagents.name: Atotech Cupracid Ultra-class acid Cu electrolyte

failure_warnings.label: Acid attack on exposed ITO at openings
failure_warnings.mitigation: Plating bath pH ≥ 0.5; minimize plating time; use pulsed current to refresh diffusion layer.

failure_warnings.label: Cu adhesion to Ni or Cu seed
failure_warnings.mitigation: Pre-plate dip in 10 % H₂SO₄ for 30 s to activate Ni/Cu surface; verify ≥2 N/mm peel before lamination per SunDrive ARENA.

protocol.title: Immersion Ag cap deposition (~200 nm)
protocol.rationale: Galvanic-displacement Ag deposition from a cyanide-free immersion bath (commercial — e.g., Atotech Bondaglas or equivalent). Ag cap protects Cu fingers from atmospheric oxidation and provides a solderable interface for downstream module interconnect. Hatt 2019 reports ~200 nm Ag cap survives 1000 h DH on glass-glass POE laminates with negligible Cu out-diffusion when paired with POE encapsulant (Karas 2022 contradiction: same cap thickness fails on EVA — encapsulant choice dominates).

failure_warnings.label: Insufficient Ag cap coverage
failure_warnings.mitigation: Verify cap continuity by EDS surface scan; minimum thickness 150 nm everywhere.

failure_warnings.label: Cu migration through Ag cap into encapsulant
failure_warnings.mitigation: Pair with POE encapsulant in step 9; Karas 2022 shows Sn/Ag caps leak Cu under EVA but not POE.

protocol.title: PVD-stack etch-back & low-T anneal
protocol.rationale: Strip the residual PVD Cu/Al cover layer (outside grid lines) using sequential selective wet etches per Hatt 2019: H₃PO₄/HNO₃ (60/3.25 wt %) at 50°C etches Al < 1 nm/s but Cu and Ag at >10 nm/s — wrong order; correct sequence is (NH₄)₂S₂O₈ 2.5 % to remove Cu seed, then NaOH 1 % to remove residual Al. Selectivity table in Hatt 2019 page 11 governs. Final low-T anneal 200°C / 15 min in air recovers a-Si:H sputter damage; do NOT exceed 200°C — Cheng 2018 shows Cu₃Si nucleation onset above this temperature.

failure_warnings.label: Over-etch into ITO
failure_warnings.mitigation: Endpoint by reflectometry; over-etch into ITO < 5 nm.

failure_warnings.label: Anneal-induced Cu₃Si nucleation
failure_warnings.mitigation: Strict ≤200°C anneal cap; never exceed 250°C in any subsequent module-process step.

protocol.title: Cell-level baseline characterization (TLM, Suns-Voc, IV, peel, EL)
protocol.rationale: All four arms (≥5 cells per arm) measured at t=0: (i) TLM contact resistivity ρc (10-line geometry, 700–4800 µm spacing per NREL Aschenbrenner 2012); (ii) Suns-Voc + dark IV for pFF and J02 (Sinton flash tester); (iii) STC IV for η, Voc, Jsc, FF (AM1.5g class AAA solar simulator); (iv) 90° peel test on a 2 mm-wide Cu/Sn ribbon soldered to a busbar — Geipel 2014 protocol, Kinloch–Kawashita adhesive fracture energy reported alongside peel force; (v) EL imaging at 1-sun current; (vi) QSSPC bulk minority-carrier lifetime on 2 sister cells per arm. Reject Arm A if ρc > 2 mΩ·cm² at t=0 — early-stop criterion.

failure_warnings.label: Baseline ρc > 2 mΩ·cm² rejects hypothesis
failure_warnings.mitigation: Re-tune PVD seed thickness or pulsed-plating waveform; iterate before lamination.

protocol.title: Mini-module lamination (POE glass-glass with PIB edge seal)
protocol.rationale: Encapsulate one cell per arm in a 2-mm low-Fe glass / 0.45 mm POE / cell / 0.45 mm POE / 2-mm glass laminate with multibusbar Cu ribbon interconnect (Pb-free SnBi solder). Apply polyisobutylene (PIB) edge seal around the perimeter. POE chosen over EVA to eliminate the acetic-acid corrosion pathway documented by Karas 2022. Vacuum-laminate at 145°C / 12 min cycle (POE-compatible profile, NOT the standard EVA 150°C/15 min). One sister Arm A laminate uses EVA — paired contrast per Karas 2022 protocol. Total: 5 laminates (4 POE arms + 1 EVA contrast).

reagents.name: EVA encapsulant film (0.45 mm) — control contrast only

failure_warnings.label: EVA hydrolysis releases acetic acid that corrodes Cu and drives Cu out-diffusion (Karas 2022 ~3× more Cu in Si vs POE)
failure_warnings.mitigation: Use POE for all hypothesis arms; the single EVA laminate is a controlled paired contrast, not the test arm.

failure_warnings.label: Edge moisture ingress into mini-module
failure_warnings.mitigation: PIB edge seal mandatory; Lachowicz 2024 identifies edge initiation as dominant DH damage mode on glass-glass laminates without seal.

protocol.title: Damp-heat aging — IEC 61215 1000 h with checkpoints
protocol.rationale: Hold all 5 laminates at 85°C / 85 % RH in a calibrated environmental chamber per IEC 61215 §10.13. Open-circuit (no electrical bias). Remove and re-measure at 250, 500, 750, 1000 h: STC IV, Suns-Voc, EL imaging at each checkpoint. Köhl 2013 Arrhenius framework: 1000 h at 85/85 maps to ~9 yr field exposure in moderate climate, ~3 yr in tropical. Baseline pass criterion: Δη ≤ 0.5 %abs at 1000 h on Arm A.

failure_warnings.label: Cu migration through Sn/Ag cap into encapsulant under DH
failure_warnings.mitigation: Karas 2022: ~5–8 % Pmax loss at 1000 h on EVA-encapsulated Cu cells; POE eliminates this. Single EVA contrast laminate is the failure-mode confirmation arm.

failure_warnings.label: Adhesion loss after thermomechanical / hydrolytic cycling
failure_warnings.mitigation: Cu seed pre-clean (HF + 10 % H₂SO₄ activation per Jeon 2017) gives +42 mV Voc; SunDrive two-step plating gives shear-strength > c-Si shear-strength.

protocol.title: Extended damp-heat 1000 → 2000 h (IEC 63209 sequence)
protocol.rationale: Continue damp-heat exposure beyond 1000 h to 2000 h, per IEC 63209 extended-stress sequence and the 2024 NREL 'Getting Ahead of the Curve' recommendation that 1000 h is no longer sufficient for SHJ-Cu. Karas 2022 shows EVA-encapsulated cells fail at 2000–3500 h while POE survives — extended DH is necessary to surface the failure mode. Re-measure at 1500 and 2000 h. Pass criterion: Δη ≤ 1.0 %abs at 2000 h on Arm A (POE).

failure_warnings.label: Cumulative encapsulant hydrolysis
failure_warnings.mitigation: Documented in EVA contrast laminate — not a defect of the hypothesis arms.

failure_warnings.label: Cu finger oxidation through Ag cap (Lachowicz 2023/2024)
failure_warnings.mitigation: Track FF and series resistance separately; FF loss without Voc loss → cap/finger issue, not Cu in Si.

protocol.title: Post-DH SIMS depth profile (Cu in c-Si verification)
protocol.rationale: Cleave one cell per arm at 0, 1000, and 2000 h DH. Run dynamic SIMS with O₂⁺ primary beam (Cs⁺ for high-mass interference cases) on a 50×50 µm raster centered under a finger. Track Cu, Ni, Sn, Ag depth profiles into c-Si. Karas 2022 detection threshold: Cu signal in c-Si > 3× the Ag-screen-print reference is diagnostic of barrier failure. Yacouba 2025 reports no detectable Cu in Si under intact TCO — this experiment falsifies or confirms that on Arm A (sub-100 nm Ni) and Arm B (no Ni) under SHJ thermal budget. Outsource to a SIMS service (CAMECA, Eurofins, or university shared facility) — ~$400/sample.

failure_warnings.label: Hypothesis-rejecting if Cu detected in c-Si bulk
failure_warnings.mitigation: If Arm A shows Cu in Si but Arm C (120 nm Ni) does not, the sub-100 nm claim fails specifically; if both fail, Cu-encapsulant pathway dominates.

protocol.title: Cost analysis & roadmap synthesis
protocol.rationale: Compute $/cell front-side metallization cost for each arm using the SunDrive ARENA process step-list (4 steps for Cu vs 1 for screen-print, but 5–10× lower material cost) and the ITRPV 2024 Ag-laydown trajectory (currently ~9 mg/cell on SHJ, projected 5 mg/cell by 2034). Sensitivity-test cost at $25/oz and $35/oz Ag spot. Goldschmidt 2021 anchors the resource-bottleneck case. Output: $/cell + $/W vs Ag baseline at 5 GW production scale, with ±20 % uncertainty band.

materials.name: Commercial SHJ M6 precursor (n-type, 155 µm, ITO 80 nm both sides, a-Si:H bilateral passivation)
materials.supplier: CSEM / Meyer Burger / LONGi pilot line

materials.name: Nickel(II) sulfamate hydrate (Ni source for sulfamate bath)
Atotech Cupracid Ultra A acid Cu plating chemistry (10 L makeup)
NaOH 99 % anhydrous (1 wt % aqueous etch ink)
POE encapsulant film 0.45 mm (200×200 mm sheets)
EVA encapsulant film 0.45 mm (control contrast only)
Low-T SHJ-grade Ag paste (Heraeus SOL954 / Solamet PV42B / Namics LCT-class)
(no purchase — failure-mode artifact tracked by SIMS / XRD)
SIMS depth-profile analysis (Cu / Ni / Sn / Ag in c-Si, 12 samples)
Sheet-resistance and TLM probe rental (Keithley 2400 SMU + 4-point head)

budget.description: 60 commercial SHJ M6 precursor cells from a pilot-line partner
Ni sulfamate, acid Cu (Atotech Cupracid Ultra), boric-acid buffer, immersion Ag cap, NaOH ink, sulfuric acid
POE & EVA films, PIB edge seal, Cu ribbon, low-Fe glass plates, low-T solder
Low-T SHJ-grade Ag paste (Heraeus SOL954 class)
PVD sputter facility (50 hrs); inkjet printer rental (Notion Systems lab unit, 4 wk); environmental chamber (85/85, 14 wk); IV / Suns-Voc / 4PP probe stations; vacuum laminator
12 SIMS depth profiles (Cu/Ni/Sn/Ag in c-Si) at 0/1000/2000 h on Arms A–D
Postdoctoral researcher (lead) + research technician (plating + measurement) at $50/hr loaded × 40 hr/wk × 14 wk × 1.5 FTE
PPE, fume hood booking, hazardous-waste disposal (acid + Ni + Cu metallics), DI water, lab supplies
Open-access publication fee, conference travel allowance (1 EU PVSEC presentation), figures and graphics
University F&A applied to direct costs
15 % contingency for failed wafer batches, equipment downtime, additional SIMS samples

timeline.name: Phase 2 · NOBLE recipe optimisation on coupons
Phase 3 · Cell metallisation (Arms A–D)
depends_on: Phase 2 · NOBLE recipe optimisation on coupons

success_criteria: Arm A (sub-100 nm Ni / Cu / Ag cap, NOBLE-style) achieves ρc ≤ 2 mΩ·cm² by TLM at t=0 (n=5 cells, σ < 0.4)
Arm A shows Δη ≤ 0.5 %abs after 1000 h DH on POE glass-glass mini-module (paired t-test vs Arm D Ag baseline, p < 0.05)
Arm A peel adhesion ≥ 1 N/mm at t=0 and t=1000 h per DIN EN 50461 / Geipel 2014
SIMS depth profile shows Cu in c-Si ≤ 3× Arm D Ag-screen-print reference background after 1000 h DH
Cost model predicts ≤ 10 % of Ag baseline $/cell at 5 GW scale, with ≥1 reproducible sensitivity point at $25/oz Ag spot
failure_criteria: Arm A ρc > 2 mΩ·cm² at t=0 — early-stop, hypothesis rejected on contact-resistivity ground
Arm A Δη > 1.0 %abs at 1000 h — hypothesis rejected on damp-heat ground
SIMS shows quantitative Cu signal in c-Si bulk on Arm A after 1000 h DH with intact TCO and Ag cap — hypothesis rejected on barrier-integrity ground
Arm A peel adhesion < 1 N/mm at any timepoint — hypothesis rejected on adhesion ground
Cost model predicts > 30 % of Ag baseline at any sensitivity point — hypothesis rejected on cost ground

measurements.method: [[wiki/methods/IV-curve-measurement]] (STC AM1.5g, class AAA simulator, calibrated reference cell)

measurements.n: 3 per arm at t=0 and t=1000 h

measurements.method: [[wiki/methods/SIMS-depth-profile]] (dynamic SIMS, O₂⁺ primary, 50×50 µm raster under finger)

measurements.n: 2 sister cells per arm at t=0, 1000, 2000 h

measurements.threshold: No new dark patches > 0.5 mm² between checkpoints

measurements.method: process step-list cost model (SunDrive ARENA + ITRPV 2024 inputs)
measurements.threshold: ≤ 10 % of Ag baseline at $25/oz Ag spot

failure_map.label: Cu diffusion into crystalline silicon
failure_map.frequency_estimate: Inevitable without barrier; sub-ppb Cu collapses lifetime (Lindroos 2015); SHJ TCO + intact Ni continuity is the hypothesis's protection
failure_map.mitigation: Sub-100 nm Ni barrier + intact TCO co-barrier; SIMS verification at every DH checkpoint; ≤200°C anneal cap to suppress Cu₃Si nucleation

failure_map.label: Cu₃Si silicide formation at Si interface
failure_map.frequency_estimate: Cheng 2018: 60 nm Ni → Cu₃Si at 300°C; 120 nm Ni → Cu₃Si at 400°C; SHJ ≤200°C bulk process budget should suppress, but lamination 145°C / 12 min and operational temperature excursions need verification
failure_map.mitigation: Hard ≤200°C cap on every post-plate thermal step; verify by XRD / STEM-EDS on coupons

failure_map.label: Ni-barrier pinholes (sub-100 nm vs Cheng threshold)
failure_map.frequency_estimate: Cheng 2018 60 nm electroplated Ni shows Cu₃Si at 300°C — the hypothesis's sub-100 nm claim is in the failure regime under high-T anneal but possibly rescued by SHJ ≤200°C process; pinhole density >10 cm⁻² rejects the wafer
failure_map.mitigation: Pinhole-decoration test (Cu displacement, 5 min) before Cu plate; reject any wafer with pinhole density >10 cm⁻²; SIMS verification post-DH

failure_map.label: EVA hydrolysis → acetic acid → Cu corrosion + out-diffusion
failure_map.frequency_estimate: Karas 2022 ~3× more Cu in Si on EVA vs POE after 3500 h DH; ~5 % Pmax loss in EVA-encapsulated cells at 2000 h; effectively eliminated by POE encapsulant + PIB edge seal
failure_map.mitigation: Use POE encapsulant for all hypothesis arms; reserve EVA for the paired contrast laminate that confirms the failure mode is encapsulant-driven

failure_map.label: Cu migration up through Sn/Ag cap into encapsulant
failure_map.frequency_estimate: Karas 2022 SIMS shows Cu reaching outer surface of Sn/Ag caps after 1000 h DH on EVA; significantly suppressed in POE
failure_map.mitigation: Ag cap ≥150 nm continuous coverage verified by EDS; Karas 2022 Cu-Ni co-deposition (~9 at% Ni in Cu) stabilizes (Cu,Ni)₆Sn₅ phase if Sn cap chosen

failure_map.label: Cu finger corrosion at encapsulant interface
failure_map.frequency_estimate: Lachowicz 2024: ~5 % Pmax loss at 2700 h on glass-glass POE without edge seal; mitigated to ≤1 % with PIB edge seal
failure_map.mitigation: PIB edge seal mandatory; lamination cycle tuned to POE (145°C / 12 min, NOT EVA 150°C / 15 min)

failure_map.label: FF degradation from contact-resistance rise
failure_map.frequency_estimate: Universal across plated-Cu cells; SunDrive achieves ρc 0.1–1 mΩ·cm², Hatt NOBLE 1.8–4.0 mΩ·cm²; ARM A target ≤2 mΩ·cm² is on the boundary
failure_map.mitigation: Optimize PVD seed thickness and bath chemistry; pulsed-reverse current per Hatt 2019 for fine-grain Cu; track ρc separately from η at every checkpoint

failure_map.label: Finger adhesion loss after damp-heat
failure_map.frequency_estimate: Historical Suntech Pluto failure mode; SunDrive achieves Si-shear-strength adhesion via two-step plating + chemistry tuning; baseline DIN EN 50461 specifies ≥1 N/mm minimum
failure_map.mitigation: HF + 10 % H₂SO₄ surface activation pre-Cu (Jeon 2017 +42 mV Voc); two-step plating with low-current strike + main fill; peel-test verification at t=0 and t=1000 h

failure_map.label: TCO (ITO) pitting at exposed plating openings
failure_map.frequency_estimate: Acid Cu pH ≈ 0.5 attacks ITO at exposed pinholes; mitigated by selective etchants (Hatt 2019) and minimal exposure time
failure_map.mitigation: Endpoint-monitor PVD-stack etch-back by reflectometry; over-etch into ITO < 5 nm; use (NH₄)₂S₂O₈ for Cu-only removal preserving ITO

failure_map.label: a-Si:H passivation degradation from sputter / anneal / UV
failure_map.frequency_estimate: Sputter damage recoverable by 200°C / 15 min anneal (Hatt 2019); fs-UV laser at >4.7 µJ damages PL signature (Hatt 2021); UV preconditioning per IEC 61215 a known stress
failure_map.mitigation: Soft-sputter mode at sample T < 80°C; mandatory 200°C / 15 min recovery anneal; UV preconditioning before DH per IEC 61215 §10.10

Nickel(II) sulfamate hydrate — Fisher / Thermo Scientific Chemicals

failure-modes/Cu-diffusion-into-c-Si.title: Cu diffusion into crystalline silicon
failure-modes/Cu-diffusion-into-c-Si.subtitle: FAILURE MODE · SEVERITY CRITICAL · the headline reason silicon PV stayed on silver for forty years
failure-modes/Cu-diffusion-into-c-Si.body:
## Why this dominates the hypothesis

Cu has the highest interstitial diffusivity of any 3d transition metal in silicon: D = 3×10⁻⁴ exp(−0.18 eV/kT) cm²/s. **A Cu finger directly on c-Si traverses a 400 µm B-doped wafer in under 11 hours at room temperature** ([[2015-lindroos-cu-lid-aalto-thesis]]). Once in the bulk Cu precipitates as η′-Cu₃Si and creates a deep recombination band between EC−0.15 and EC−0.58 eV that collapses minority-carrier lifetime catastrophically.

## What the literature says about the barrier

- **Cheng 2018** ([[2018-electrochemsci-barrier-properties-electroplated-ni]]): STEM-EDS confirmed 60 nm electroplated Ni → Cu₃Si formation at 300°C; 120 nm Ni holds to 300°C, fails at 400°C.
- **Bartsch 2010** ([[2010-bartsch-lip-pvsc]]): Arrhenius extrapolation predicts 0.1 yr lifetime without Ni vs >1000 yr with optimized Ni-Cu-Sn stack.
- **Yacouba 2025** ([[2025-yacouba-shj-silver-free-metallization-progPV]]): SIMS finds NO detectable Cu in c-Si on intact-TCO SHJ even with direct Cu-paste contact. **The SHJ TCO itself is a viable Cu barrier when intact.**
- **SunDrive ARENA** ([[2022-arena-sundrive-copper-metallisation-demonstration]]): 26.41 % certified SHJ with NO Ni barrier — relies entirely on TCO + adhesion-tuned chemistry.

## How this experiment falsifies or confirms

Arm A (sub-100 nm Ni) sits at the edge of Cheng's threshold. SHJ's ≤200°C process budget likely rescues it but the experiment's SIMS at 0/1000/2000 h on Arm A vs Arm C (120 nm Ni) directly maps the threshold. Arm B (no Ni, TCO-only) tests the SunDrive route on the same wafer batch.

## Failure decision rule

If SIMS shows quantitative Cu signal in c-Si bulk on Arm A after 1000 h DH while Arm D (Ag baseline) shows none → hypothesis rejected on barrier-integrity ground.

failure-modes/EVA-acetic-acid-corrosion.title: EVA hydrolysis → acetic acid → Cu out-diffusion
failure-modes/EVA-acetic-acid-corrosion.subtitle: FAILURE MODE · SEVERITY HIGH · the encapsulant decision dominates module reliability
failure-modes/EVA-acetic-acid-corrosion.body:
## Mechanism

Ethylene-vinyl-acetate hydrolyzes under hot/humid conditions to release acetic acid. Inside a laminate at 85°C / 85 % RH, the acid attacks the Cu / Sn / Ag cap stack, mobilizes Cu via grain-boundary diffusion, and ultimately drives Cu *back into c-Si* through the encapsulant via lateral edge migration.

## What Karas 2022 measured

[[2022-karas-cu-outdiffusion-damp-heat]] paired matched plated-Cu cells laminated in EVA vs POE and ran 0 → 3500 h DH with SIMS at every checkpoint. Result: **~3× more Cu at the Si surface in EVA samples**; pFF dropped 4–5 %abs in EVA, no loss in POE. EDS cross-sections show Cu lining voids in the EVA above the fingers — the encapsulant becomes a Cu reservoir.

## Mitigation tier-list

1. **Use POE encapsulant** — eliminates the acetic-acid pathway. SHJ industry default per [[2024-nrel-getting-ahead-of-curve-pv-assessment]].
2. **PIB edge seal** — Lachowicz 2024 identifies edge-initiated moisture ingress as the dominant DH damage mode on glass-glass laminates without seal.
3. **Extended DH (2000 h)** — 1000 h is no longer sufficient for SHJ-Cu reliability claims (NREL 2024).
4. **Ni co-deposition in Cu** (~9 at% Ni) stabilizes (Cu,Ni)₆Sn₅ phase if Sn cap is used.

## Why this experiment includes an EVA contrast laminate

One paired Arm A laminate is intentionally encapsulated in EVA to *prove* the failure mechanism is encapsulant-driven, not architecture-driven. If Arm A passes on POE but the EVA contrast fails, the hypothesis is supported and the encapsulant choice is the dominant lever for industrial deployment.

methods/NOBLE-selective-Cu-plating.title: NOBLE — Native Oxide Barrier Layer for Electroplating
methods/NOBLE-selective-Cu-plating.subtitle: METHOD · Fraunhofer ISE flagship SHJ-Cu route · resist-free, inkjet-driven
methods/NOBLE-selective-Cu-plating.body:
## What NOBLE does

A thin PVD Cu/Al stack is deposited on the SHJ ITO. The Al's self-passivating native AlOx (formed by 1–5 d ambient hold) acts as the plating mask — **eliminating the organic resist** used by CSEM's 7-step route. Inkjet-printed 1 wt% NaOH(aq) (2–5 pL droplets) selectively etches Al only along the desired grid lines, exposing the Cu seed. Cu and Ag are then electroplated only into the openings; residual PVD Cu/Al outside the grid is etched back with selective wet chemistry (H₃PO₄/HNO₃ for Al; (NH₄)₂S₂O₈ for Cu).

## Demonstrated performance

- [[2019-hatt-noble-shj-solrrl]] — first lab cell: 20.2 % efficiency, FF 78.0 %, ρc 1.8–4.0 mΩ·cm² to ITO
- [[2019-hatt-noble-bifacial-shj-aip]] — full-area 156×156 mm² demonstration at 20.0 %, contact-angle evolution detailed
- [[2021-hatt-stable-cu-plated-shj-eupvsec]] — 12 cells reproducibly **22.6 %** on M2; ρc 0.1–1 mΩ·cm² with optimized seed; ≥2 N/mm peel adhesion; fs-UV laser variant 21.4 %

## Key operating window

- Inkjet: 1 wt% NaOH(aq), 2–5 pL droplets, AlOx contact angle 100–120°.
- Plating: pulsed forward/reverse, 6 A/dm², 15 ms forward / 1 ms reverse, anodic:cathodic 4.5.
- Final stack: PVD-Cu(50 nm) / plated-Cu(1–10 µm) / immersion-Ag(~200 nm).
- Anneal cap: 200°C / 15 min in air to recover a-Si:H sputter damage.

## Why this experiment uses NOBLE

NOBLE is the most documented public SHJ-Cu route with an open process recipe. Steps 2–7 of the protocol are essentially the NOBLE flow plus an inserted Ni electroplate step (Arms A and C). Arm B drops the Ni step and reverts to the literal NOBLE recipe — this is the SunDrive-comparable arm.

hypothesis.title: Hypothesis · sub-100 nm Ni / Cu electroplated SHJ
hypothesis.subtitle: ROOT NODE · status: complete · 4-arm comparison study
hypothesis.body:
## The literal hypothesis

On silicon heterojunction (SHJ) solar cells, a sub-100 nm electroplated nickel barrier followed by electroplated copper produces a front-grid metallization with contact resistivity ≤ 2 mΩ·cm² and no measurable efficiency loss after 1000 h damp-heat aging (85°C / 85% RH) — matching screen-printed silver at <10% of the metallization cost.

## What the experiment actually tests

A 4-arm comparison on the same commercial SHJ M6 wafer batch:

| Arm | Stack | Tests |
|---|---|---|
| **A** | PVD-Cu/Al → inkjet-NaOH → **Ni 80 nm** → Cu 5 µm → Ag cap | The literal hypothesis |
| **B** | PVD-Cu → inkjet-NaOH → (no Ni) → Cu 5 µm → Ag cap | SunDrive-style: TCO-only barrier |
| **C** | PVD-Cu/Al → inkjet-NaOH → **Ni 120 nm** → Cu 5 µm → Ag cap | Cheng 2018 safe-threshold reference |
| **D** | Screen-printed low-T SHJ-grade Ag paste | Industry baseline control |

All four arms laminated in **POE glass-glass mini-modules with PIB edge seal**. One paired Arm A laminate uses EVA as a controlled contrast to prove the encapsulant-driven failure mode (Karas 2022).

## Why this beats a simple replication

- Arms A vs C **directly map the Cheng 2018 60 nm threshold under SHJ ≤200°C process budget** — a measurement no published study has done.
- Arms A vs B isolate whether the Ni barrier adds value on SHJ (TCO is itself a barrier per Yacouba 2025 / SunDrive).
- EVA vs POE matched contrast at 2000 h gives an **encapsulant decision rule** that the cost case depends on.
- Cost model anchored to ITRPV 2024 + SunDrive ARENA gives a defensible $/cell number that Hatt and Lachowicz do not publish.
